summary: CC-0108 closes the admin-credential rotation gap by making the bootstrap Job's identity depend on the admin password content. internal/common/job/job.go now hashes the full corev1.PodTemplateSpec (PodSpecHash(&job.Spec.Template)) instead of only PodSpec, so pod-template annotations participate in the forge.c5c3.io/pod-spec-hash delete-and-recreate gate; all RunJob/createJobWithHash call sites and every test helper (job_test.go, reconcile_database_test.go, reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go, keystone_controller_test.go, database_test.go) were updated transparently. reconcileBootstrap reads the admin Secret's `password` via secrets.GetSecretValue, computes hex(sha256(password)), and buildBootstrapJob stamps it on Template.ObjectMeta.Annotations["forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash"]; a missing/unreadable Secret, absent key, or empty value sets BootstrapReady=False/AdminSecretInvalid, emits a Warning event, and returns the error before any Job is built. Coverage spans all tiers: new unit tests (job + bootstrap), envtest integration tests (rotation cutover + no-churn, both PASS under envtest), and a Chainsaw E2E suite proving old-password 401 / new-password 201 with stable API pod UIDs. Docs (reconciler reference + new operator guide) are accurate and cross-linked. No API/CRD/generated artifacts changed (REQ-006), no controller-side OpenBao client added (REQ-012), and the architecture/ submodule is untouched. Verified locally: go build/vet clean, all unit tests green, both rotation integration tests PASS, golangci-lint clean on CC-0108 files (the only 2 lint hits are in rotation_validation.go and reconcile_credential_test.go, both pre-existing and out of scope). Findings are minor only.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass (job primitive + reconcileBootstrap)
Integration (envtest) tests pass: AdminPasswordRotationCutover + AdminPasswordUnchangedNoChurn (verified PASS locally)
E2E Chainsaw suite present and auto-discovered by CI (401/201 cutover, BootstrapReady=True, no Deployment rollout)
Edge cases tested: missing `password` key and empty `password` value (REQ-005)
BootstrapReady cutover (False/BootstrapInProgress -> True/BootstrapComplete) verified at unit + integration tiers (REQ-004, REQ-010)
All existing Job-hash test helpers updated to PodSpecHash(&...Template); whole Go suite green
Dedicated test for an entirely-absent admin Secret (same error branch as missing-key, but not separately asserted)
No-churn test asserts metadata.resourceVersion unchanged as the AC text literally states (asserts metadata.uid only)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing patterns: pod-spec-hash gate, sub-reconciler condition contract (5 fields incl. ObservedGeneration), event-on-transition convention
No production code duplication; hash/sha256 logic lives once in reconcileBootstrap
Functions remain single-responsibility; buildBootstrapJob extended by one parameter
Implementation-time 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION notes resolved after test coverage (3 remain)

security_checklist.item: Admin password is not logged, nor exposed in events or error messages (messages reference Secret name/key only)
Unsalted SHA-256-in-annotation is an accepted change-comparator per REQ-011 (password is high-entropy, OpenBao-generated)
//nolint:gosec on the annotation key const is justified and documented (key, not a credential)
E2E auth probe (kubectl exec python3 urllib) passes the password as argv (not interpolated into a shell string); no TLS-verification bypass

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal — reuses the existing RunJob delete-and-recreate-on-hash-divergence gate rather than adding new machinery
No new spec fields, CRD, deepcopy, or Helm CRD changes (REQ-006); Model B correctly deferred
No controller-side OpenBao client; Part 1 reads only the in-cluster Secret (REQ-012)
Shared internal/common/job signature change: blast radius checked — all call sites and test helpers updated; only keystone consumes RunJob
Secret watch (keystoneSecretNameExtractor) already indexes adminPasswordSecretRef.name, so rotation is event-driven (confirmed end-to-end by envtest)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No speculative Model B scaffolding (passwordRotation spec/CronJob/PushSecret) — explicitly out of scope

fail_fast_checklist.item: Missing/empty admin password fails fast: condition set + Warning event + error returned before any Job is built

defensive_checklist.item: Admin Secret read is defended against missing-secret/missing-key/empty-value even though reconcileSecrets gates SecretsReady upstream (defense-in-depth)

issues_found.description: Three implementation-time DECISION comments still carry the 'Reviewer: please verify ...' sign-off request. Per the project's external-review pattern (.planwerk/review_patterns/resolve-ambiguous-commented-out-alternatives-before-merge.md, CC-0065), these notes should be resolved/removed once the documented behavior is codified and covered by tests. All three behaviors are now fully tested and passing, so the verify request is stale. Keep the DECISION rationale; drop the 'Reviewer: please verify' line.
issues_found.fix: Remove the 'Reviewer: please verify ...' sentence from each of the three DECISION blocks. The decisions are sound: REQ-001 (hash full PodTemplateSpec) is covered by TestPodSpecHash_TemplateAnnotationParticipates / TestRunJob_RecreatesOnTemplateAnnotationChange / TestBuildBootstrapJob_AdminPasswordHashAnnotation; REQ-005 (error on missing/empty password) by TestReconcileBootstrap_MissingPasswordKeyCleanError / TestReconcileBootstrap_EmptyPasswordCleanError; REQ-009 (dedicated admin ExternalSecret for isolation) by the chainsaw suite.
issues_found.evidence: job.go:43 `// Reviewer: please verify this matches intent (CC-0108, REQ-001).`  |  reconcile_bootstrap.go:64 `// Reviewer: please verify this matches intent (CC-0108, REQ-005).`  |  00-keystone-cr.yaml:26 `# Reviewer: please verify this isolation choice (CC-0108, REQ-009).`

issues_found.description: The no-churn invariant (REQ-003) acceptance-criterion text states the test 'asserts the Job's metadata.uid AND metadata.resourceVersion are unchanged after the reconcile'. The unit test (TestReconcileBootstrap_UnchangedPasswordRetainsJob) and the integration test (TestIntegration_AdminPasswordUnchangedNoChurn) assert metadata.uid stability only; neither asserts metadata.resourceVersion. The invariant is still functionally proven — UID stability rules out delete/recreate and RunJob returns early without any in-place write when the hash matches — so this is a test-completeness nuance rather than a coverage gap, but it diverges from the literal AC.
issues_found.fix: Capture the completed Job's resourceVersion before the reconcile and assert it is unchanged afterwards (alongside the existing UID assertion) so the test matches the REQ-003 acceptance-criterion wording verbatim and additionally guards against any future spurious in-place Job Update.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_bootstrap_test.go:886 `g.Expect(retained.UID).To(Equal(completed.UID),` ... (no resourceVersion assertion). AC text (REQ-003): 'the test asserts the Job's metadata.uid and metadata.resourceVersion are unchanged after the reconcile.'

issues_found.description: reconcileBootstrap is tested for an admin Secret that exists-but-lacks-the-key and for an empty value, but not for an entirely-absent admin Secret object. Note this is defense-in-depth, not a true coverage gap: a completely missing Secret makes secrets.GetSecretValue return a wrapped NotFound error, which flows through the exact same `if err != nil` branch (reconcile_bootstrap.go:81-84) that TestReconcileBootstrap_MissingPasswordKeyCleanError already exercises via ErrKeyNotFound. The reconcileBootstrap branch is therefore covered; only an explicit fixture for the missing-object case is absent.
issues_found.fix: Optionally add TestReconcileBootstrap_MissingAdminSecretCleanError that builds the reconciler WITHOUT the admin Secret and asserts: error returned, BootstrapReady=False/AdminSecretInvalid, Warning event emitted, no bootstrap Job created — mirroring the two existing clean-error tests for explicitness.
issues_found.evidence: reconcile_bootstrap_test.go has TestReconcileBootstrap_MissingPasswordKeyCleanError (key absent) and TestReconcileBootstrap_EmptyPasswordCleanError (empty value); no test omits the Secret object. Both existing tests and the absent-Secret case share the `if err != nil` path at reconcile_bootstrap.go:81.

suggested_improvements: The Chainsaw E2E suite waits for BootstrapReady to return to True after rotation but does not assert it passed through False/BootstrapInProgress; capturing a transient condition in E2E is inherently racy and is already deterministically verified by the unit test TestReconcileBootstrap_CutoverConditionTransitions and the envtest integration test, so leaving E2E as-is is reasonable.

Consider extracting the repeated hex(sha256(password)) computation used across the test helpers (bootstrapAdminPasswordHash, hashOf, integration_test, keystone_controller_test) into a single shared test helper to keep the digest derivation defined in one place.

The `password` data-key literal appears in the GetSecretValue call, the error messages, and the env SecretKeyRef in reconcile_bootstrap.go; a small package-level const would centralize it (matches the pattern of extracting repeated literals), though it is consistent with the pre-existing SecretKeyRef usage.
next_steps: Resolve the three 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION sign-off notes (job.go:43, reconcile_bootstrap.go:64, 00-keystone-cr.yaml:26) now that all referenced behaviors are codified and tested.
Add a metadata.resourceVersion-unchanged assertion to the no-churn unit/integration tests to match the REQ-003 acceptance-criterion wording verbatim.
Optionally add an explicit missing-admin-Secret-object error test for completeness (the underlying branch is already covered by the missing-key test).
